cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking for the purpose of the author's "loud thank you" to the doorman. The options are A through D. I need to analyze the text to determine which is correct. First, let me recap the story. The author and her family are on their way to California and stop for lunch. At the restaurant, a man (referred to as the doorman) opens the door for them and greets them. The kids comment on his smell, and the author tells them to look beyond the dirt. She feels upset by their rudeness and wants to be a good example. Later, when she goes back to get car-sick pills, the doorman is opening the door again, and she says a loud thank you. Then she buys him food, explaining to her family, and the doorman becomes very grateful. The key part here is why the author said a loud "thank you" when she was going out to get the pills. Let's look at the text around that moment. It says: "When I returned, we talked a bit. He said he was not allowed inside unless he bought food." So the author then asks the waitress to add a soup and sandwich for the doorman. The kids are puzzled but smile when they hear it's for him. The author's actions—saying a loud thank you and then getting food—seem to be part of a lesson in gratitude. The story ends with the author reflecting that while we can't choose many things in life, we can choose when to show gratitude. She mentions that she thanked the man for opening the door and that it was also an opportunity to teach her children by example. So the "thank you" was not just a polite manner (option D), but part of a larger lesson. The loud "thank you" might have been intentional to set an example in front of her children, leading them to understand the importance of gratitude. The subsequent action of buying food for the doorman reinforces this, as the author explains the situation to her family, making it clear that the "thank you" was a lesson in gratitude. Looking at the options: A. She wanted to give the children a lesson of gratitude. B. She showed her real love for him. (The story doesn't mention love, more gratitude and teaching.) C. She wanted to give the doorman a hand. (The doorman is the one helping them by opening the door. The giving a hand would be buying him food later, not the initial thank you.) D. She just showed her polite manner. (The story suggests it was more than just polite; it was part of a lesson.) The
